danger_signals.visual_form: Player animations showing shoving and elbowing during play
danger_signals.meaning: Defensive pressure; risk of losing ball or being knocked back

danger_signals.visual_form: Score display showing lower team points than opponent
danger_signals.meaning: Losing game state; increased urgency to score

danger_signals.visual_form: Player movement speed reduction, slower jump animations

safety_signals.visual_form: Player sprite renders with ball graphic in hands
Visual effects and announcer callouts indicating streak status
safety_signals.meaning: Peak performance; temporary boost to shooting accuracy and power

safety_signals.visual_form: Score display showing higher team points than opponent
safety_signals.meaning: Winning position; control of game state

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary team color palette (varies by NBA team selection)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team logo on jersey and court side
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent team rendered in contrasting color scheme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team bench on left side of court; team colors in scoreboard border

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rendered in distinct color from home team
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team bench on right side of court; opponent colors in court graphics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Neutral court lighting; balanced scoreboard display; default player animation sets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard movement speed; normal shooting arcs; baseline difficulty AI patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Potential visual intensity increase through announcer emphasis (Tim Kitzrow callouts)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent AI aggression increases; faster player movements; more aggressive defensive positioning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scoreboard emphasizes gap; visual celebration cues from leading team
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Trailing team AI becomes more aggressive; increased pressure on ball handler

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player glows or visual effects around shooting player; announcer calls out streak status
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased shot accuracy; longer range effectiveness; faster animation pace

environmental_storytelling.example: NBA team logos and colors embedded in court floor design communicate league legitimacy and specific franchise identity
Home and away benches positioned on opposite sides of court establishes spatial team territories and reinforces competitive opposition
Dynamic scoreboard updates reflect real-time game state progression and communicate winning/losing conditions
Ability to select from NBA-licensed real players communicates authenticity of league and creates recognizable competitive matchups
Tim Kitzrow announcer callouts synchronized with on-screen action create narrative momentum and emphasize critical plays

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can jump exaggerated heights; performs slam dunks with unrealistic power; moves at standard pace across court
Increased shooting range; higher shot success probability; faster animation cycles; glowing visual effects
Reduced movement speed; lower jump height; slower animation playback; decreased defensive responsiveness
More aggressive defensive pressure; increased shoving/elbowing attempts; tighter coverage of ball handler
More conservative positioning; defensive focus on preventing opponent scores; reduced reckless aggression
Increased shot attempts; higher three-point range attempts; more aggressive drive-to-basket plays

notes: NBA Jam (1993/Genesis) communicates world-state primarily through: (1) Scoreboard/numerical feedback; (2) Player animation pacing and visual effects; (3) Announcer audio cues (Tim Kitzrow callouts); (4) Team color differentiation; (5) On-screen HUD elements. The game lacks complex faction politics—instead using binary sports opposition (home vs. away team). Danger is communicated through score deficit and opponent positioning rather than explicit visual hazards. The 'on-fire' mechanic serves as the primary performance-state communication system, conveyed through both visual effects and announcer emphasis. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on NBA licensing authenticity and court/jersey branding rather than narrative world-building.
